meta.name: Split Pea Soup (Smoked Turkey)
meta.subtitle: Classic comfort soup • smoky, hearty • supports smoked turkey or lamb options

source.notes: Designed for SSA batch cooking + freezer use. Includes smoked turkey baseline with optional smoked lamb swap and meatless fallback.

yield.notes: Makes ~14–16 cups depending on simmer and thickness.

ingredients.label: Green split peas, rinsed and picked over
Chicken or turkey broth (low-sodium preferred)
ingredients.notes: About 8 1/2 cups. Use water if needed; adjust salt at the end.

ingredients.label: Water (optional, adjust thickness)
ingredients.notes: Add during simmer if soup thickens too much.

ingredients.label: Smoked turkey leg/drumstick/wing (bone-in)
ingredients.notes: 1–2 pieces. Add whole for simmer; shred and return meat to soup.

ingredients.label: Smoked lamb (optional swap for turkey)
ingredients.notes: Use smoked lamb neck/bones/chunks. Swap in place of smoked turkey.

ingredients.label: Salt (adjust to broth and smoked meat)
ingredients.notes: Start light; smoked meats vary widely in salt.

ingredients.label: Apple cider vinegar or lemon juice (optional, for brightness)

steps.title: Sweat the aromatics
instructions: Heat oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add onion, carrots, and celery. Cook 8–10 minutes, stirring, until softened.
Add garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant.

steps.title: Add peas, broth, and smoked meat
instructions: Add rinsed split peas, broth, bay leaves, thyme, black pepper, and a light pinch of salt.
Nestle smoked turkey into the pot (or smoked lamb if using).
Bring to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to maintain a steady simmer.

steps.title: Simmer until peas are creamy
instructions: Simmer partially covered 60–75 minutes, stirring occasionally, until split peas are very tender and the soup thickens.
If the soup becomes too thick, add water a little at a time.

steps.title: Shred smoked turkey (or lamb) and return to soup
instructions: Remove smoked turkey from the pot and let cool briefly.
Pull meat from bones, shred into bite-size pieces, and discard skin/bones (or reserve bones for stock).
Return shredded meat to the pot and stir.

steps.title: Adjust texture and seasoning
instructions: For a smoother texture, blend 1–2 cups of soup with an immersion blender and stir back in (optional).
Taste and adjust salt and pepper.
Add a small splash of vinegar or lemon juice (optional) to brighten.
Serve hot; garnish with parsley if desired.

bestPractices: Cool quickly and portion for freezer. Soup thickens when chilled; loosen with broth/water when reheating.
If you prefer distinct chunks, skip blending; if you prefer creamy, blend a portion.

useWhatYouHaveLogic.notes: If no smoked meat: add 2g smoked paprika and a bit more salt; optionally add 10g soy sauce or bouillon for depth.

storage.fridge: Refrigerate up to 5 days. Thickens as it sits; add broth/water to loosen.
storage.freezer: Freeze up to 3 months. Thaw overnight and reheat gently, adding liquid as needed.
storage.reheat: Reheat to a simmer, stirring often to prevent sticking.

notes: Split peas can scorch as the soup thickens—stir more frequently in the last 15–20 minutes.
Smoked meats vary in salt; always season at the end after tasting.
For a thinner, brothy version, increase broth by 250–500g and reduce simmer time slightly.

pairings.notes: Great with a simple salad or pickled vegetables for contrast.

audit.testNotes: Schema-compliant: schemaVersion+meta present; ingredients include id+label+amount; steps include id+title+instructions. Optional smoked lamb swap provided.
